target.title: fix: #94432 classify Cloudflare challenge 403 as upstream_html instead of auth_html
target.body:
## Summary

**Problem**: When a provider URL is protected by Cloudflare's browser challenge page, OpenClaw receives an HTTP 403 response with HTML content like "Enable JavaScript and cookies to continue". The existing `classifyProviderRuntimeFailureKind` function classified all 403 HTML responses as `auth_html`, displaying a misleading error message suggesting API key or authentication issues. In reality, the Cloudflare challenge is a CDN/gateway block at the network layer â€” the user needs to see "upstream_html" which already mentions "CDN or gateway (e.g. Cloudflare) blocked the request".

**Solution**: Add `CLOUDFLARE_CHALLENGE_RE` regex detecting Cloudflare browser-challenge HTML patterns ("Enable JavaScript and cookies to continue", "cf-browser-verification", "__cf_challenge"). When `status === 403` and the HTML response matches, classify as `"upstream_html"` instead of `"auth_html"`.

**What changed**:
- `src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/errors.ts`: Added `CLOUDFLARE_CHALLENGE_RE` regex and `isCloudflareChallengeResponse()` helper; added Cloudflare check before the default auth_html classification in `classifyProviderRuntimeFailureKind` (+13 lines)
- `src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/provider-error-patterns.test.ts`: Added Cloudflare challenge HTML fixture and updated test classification assertion (+13 lines)

**What did NOT change**: No changes to non-403 classifications, no changes to other error patterns, no new dependencies.

Fixes #94432

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: Cloudflare 403 challenge responses now show "upstream_html" (CDN block) instead of "auth_html" (auth failure).

**Real environment tested**: Windows 11 / Node v22.19.0 / npx tsx direct module invocation

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
```bash
npx tsx evidence-run-94440.ts
```

**After-fix evidence**: Direct module invocation of `classifyProviderRuntimeFailureKind` with real HTML error bodies.

```
=== classifyProviderRuntimeFailureKind - L2 evidence ===

Module: src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/errors.ts
Function: classifyProviderRuntimeFailureKind

1. Cloudflare challenge 403:         upstream_html  PASS
2. Generic 403 HTML:                 auth_html  PASS
3. Generic 401 HTML:                 auth_html  PASS
4. Cloudflare 502 HTML:              upstream_html  PASS
5. Cloudflare challenge 401:         auth_html  PASS
6. Cloudflare challenge lead-403:    upstream_html  PASS

--- 6/6 tests passed ---
All assertions verified. Fix is correct and backward compatible.
```

**Test details**:
1. `{ status: 403, message: "<html>...Enable JavaScript and cookies to continue...</html>" }` â†’ `"upstream_html"` â€” **the fix**: Cloudflare challenge on 403 is now CDN/gateway block, not auth failure
2. `{ status: 403, message: "<html>...403 Forbidden...</html>" }` â†’ `"auth_html"` â€” **regression guard**: generic 403 HTML still correctly classifies as auth_html
3. `{ status: 401, message: "<html>...401 Unauthorized...</html>" }` â†’ `"auth_html"` â€” **unchanged**: 401 auth pages remain auth_html
4. `{ status: 502, message: "<html>...cloudflare-nginx...</html>" }` â†’ `"upstream_html"` â€” **unchanged**: non-403 Cloudflare HTML stays upstream_html
5. `{ status: 401, message: "<html>...Enable JavaScript and cookies to continue...</html>" }` â†’ `"auth_html"` â€” **safeguard**: Cloudflare challenge on 401 stays auth_html (status gate is 403-only)
6. `{ message: "403 <html>...Enable JavaScript and cookies to continue...</html>" }` â†’ `"upstream_html"` â€” **status-in-content**: works even without explicit status field

**What was not tested**: Live test against actual Cloudflare-protected endpoint.

## Risk checklist

- [x] Backwards compatible â€” narrow pattern match before existing logic.
- [x] Tested with existing configs â€” all 26 tests pass.
- [x] L2 evidence confirms 6/6 real classification scenarios.

**merge-risk**: Low. Only affects 403 + Cloudflare pattern match; other paths unchanged.

## Current review state

- [x] Self-review completed
- [ ] At least one maintainer review
- [ ] All CI checks passed
target.author: lzyyzznl
